page: Amazon Web Services m5n.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The configuration provides 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. Memory capacity stands at 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high memory bandwidth. The multi-core stress-ng benchmark and Passmark CPU score both rank in the top 25% tier, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. Memory read bandwidth reaches 4502.67 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 215.88 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, which also ranks in the top 25%. Single-core performance, however, is moderate, with Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core tests placing in the middle 50% tier.

The instance is equipped with a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, making it highly efficient for network-heavy operations despite the lack of local NVMe storage. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high resource density of 96 vCPUs and 384 GB RAM combined with high-speed networking provides a balanced profile for enterprise workloads without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. It is highly suited for database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it delivers a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, placing in the top 25% tier, alongside strong Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. Conversely, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are average to weak. This hardware profile is highly suited for network-intensive workloads, database operations, caching layers, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.24xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-throughput database and networking workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 384 GB RAM optimized for network-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network throughput
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance in the top 25% tier
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving
